name: Wisdom for Parenting
keywords: how to be a good parent
note: No parent feels fully qualified — and God knows that. He promises wisdom generously to anyone who asks, and He entrusted your children to you on purpose. Parent from His strength, not your own perfection.

verses.text: But if any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask of God, who gives to all liberally and without reproach; and it will be given to him.
Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.

These words, which I command you today, shall be on your heart; and you shall teach them diligently to your children, and shall talk of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk by the way, and when you lie down, and when you rise up.
Behold, children are a heritage of Yahweh. The fruit of the womb is his reward.
He will feed his flock like a shepherd. He will gather the lambs in his arm, and carry them in his bosom. He will gently lead those who have their young.
being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ.

name: When Kids Keep Fighting
note: Sibling conflict is one of the oldest stories in the Bible — and one of the best classrooms for grace. These verses help children (and parents) practice peacemaking, patience, and quick forgiveness. Unity at home is something God delights to build, one small reconciliation at a time.

verses.text: See how good and how pleasant it is for brothers to live together in unity!
And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.
bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.
Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.

name: Disciplining with Patience
note: God disciplines the children He loves — patiently, purposefully, and never in rage. These passages model correction that trains rather than crushes, and they offer grace for the parent who is still learning self-control too.

verses.text: Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.
You fathers, don’t provoke your children to wrath, but nurture them in the discipline and instruction of the Lord.
All chastening seems for the present to be not joyous but grievous; yet afterward it yields the peaceful fruit of righteousness to those who have been exercised thereby.
So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger; for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.

name: Praying for Your Children
note: The most powerful parenting happens on your knees. God hears every prayer you pray over your children, and He loves them even more than you do. These verses are promises to pray back to Him, by name, for each child.

verses.text: All your children will be taught by Yahweh; and your children’s peace will be great.
I have no greater joy than this, to hear about my children walking in truth.
being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ.
Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.
having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you; which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.
Jesus summoned them, saying, “Allow the little children to come to me, and don’t hinder them, for God’s Kingdom belongs to such as these.

name: Struggling in Marriage
note: Every marriage walks through hard seasons, and God does not waste them. He is the third strand in the cord — able to soften hearts, rebuild trust, and renew love that feels worn thin. Keep turning toward each other, and keep turning together toward Him.

verses.text: If a man prevails against one who is alone, two shall withstand him; and a threefold cord is not quickly broken.

Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud, doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil; doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
Above all these things, walk in love, which is the bond of perfection.
And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.
with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love; being eager to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.

name: Communication in Marriage
note: Most marriage wounds are word-shaped — and so is much of the healing. Scripture coaches us to listen first, soften our answers, and speak words that build. Small changes in tone can change the temperature of a whole home.

verses.text: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.
Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.
He who gives answer before he hears, that is folly and shame to him.

name: Forgiveness in Marriage
note: Two imperfect people under one roof will need oceans of forgiveness — and God supplies it. He forgave us completely, which becomes both our model and our power source. Forgiveness in marriage is rarely one decision; it is a daily direction.

verses.text: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.
And above all things be earnest in your love among yourselves, for love covers a multitude of sins.
Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander, be put away from you, with all malice. And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
“For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.

Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud, doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil; doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
It is because of Yahweh’s loving kindnesses that we are not consumed, because his compassion doesn’t fail. They are new every morning. Great is your faithfulness.

name: Rekindling Love
note: Love in Scripture is less a feeling that finds us than a practice that forms us — patient, kind, persevering. As you serve, honor, and delight in one another again, feelings often follow faithfulness. God loves to renew what time has worn.

verses.text: Many waters can’t quench love, neither can floods drown it. If a man would give all the wealth of his house for love, he would be utterly scorned.

Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud, doesn’t behave itself inappropriately, doesn’t seek its own way, is not provoked, takes no account of evil; doesn’t rejoice in unrighteousness, but rejoices with the truth; bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor preferring one another;

Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labor. For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow; but woe to him who is alone when he falls, and doesn’t have another to lift him up.

doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself; each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.
We love him, because he first loved us.

name: Forgiving Someone Who Hurt You
note: Forgiveness does not excuse the wrong or erase the wound — it releases the debt into God's hands, where justice and mercy both live. It is often slow, and that is okay. God forgave us everything, and He will help you forgive this.

verses.text: bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.
“For if you forgive men their trespasses, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander, be put away from you, with all malice. And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
Don’t seek revenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to God’s wrath. For it is written, “Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, says the Lord.”
As far as the east is from the west, so far has he removed our transgressions from us.
Jesus said, “Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing.” Dividing his garments among them, they cast lots.

name: When You Need to Apologize
note: A real apology is one of the bravest and most healing acts a person can offer. Scripture urges us to make things right quickly and humbly — with people and with God, who forgives fully the moment we confess.

verses.text: “If therefore you are offering your gift at the altar, and there remember that your brother has anything against you, leave your gift there before the altar, and go your way. First be reconciled to your brother, and then come and offer your gift.
If we confess our sins, he is faithful and righteous to forgive us the sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
Confess your offenses to one another, and pray for one another, that you may be healed. The insistent prayer of a righteous person is powerfully effective.
He who conceals his sins doesn’t prosper, but whoever confesses and renounces them finds mercy.
Create in me a clean heart, O God. Renew a right spirit within me.
Zacchaeus stood and said to the Lord, “Behold, Lord, half of my goods I give to the poor. If I have wrongfully exacted anything of anyone, I restore four times as much.”

name: Resolving a Conflict
note: Peacemaking is family business in God's house — Jesus called peacemakers His Father's children. These verses give a path: go directly, speak gently, listen fully, and pursue peace as far as it depends on you. Some conflicts take time; keep your side of the bridge open.

verses.text: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called children of God.
If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
“If your brother sins against you, go, show him his fault between you and him alone. If he listens to you, you have gained back your brother.

But the wisdom that is from above is first pure, then peaceful, gentle, reasonable, full of mercy and good fruits, without partiality, and without hypocrisy. Now the fruit of righteousness is sown in peace by those who make peace.
The beginning of strife is like breaching a dam, therefore stop contention before quarreling breaks out.

name: Letting Go of Bitterness
note: Bitterness promises protection but quietly poisons the one who carries it. God invites you to hand Him the record of wrongs and let His kindness soften what has gone hard. Letting go is not weakness — it is freedom.

verses.text: looking carefully lest there be any man who falls short of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble you, and many be defiled by it;
Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander, be put away from you, with all malice. And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.
He who covers an offense promotes love; but he who repeats a matter separates best friends.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath. Don’t fret, it leads only to evildoing.
casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.

Finally, brothers, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are of good report; if there is any virtue, and if there is any praise, think about these things.

name: Anxiety & Worry
note: God does not scold the anxious heart — He invites it closer. Cast every care on Him, piece by piece, and let His peace stand guard where worry used to pace. You were never meant to carry tomorrow today.

verses.text: In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.
casting all your worries on him, because he cares for you.
Therefore don’t be anxious for tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Each day’s own evil is sufficient.
Cast your burden on Yahweh, and he will sustain you. He will never allow the righteous to be moved.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
Peace I leave with you. My peace I give to you; not as the world gives, give I to you. Don’t let your heart be troubled, neither let it be fearful.

name: Fear of the Future
note: The future is unknown to you, but it is not unknown to God — and He is already there. His plans for His people are good, and His presence goes ahead of every step. You can walk into tomorrow holding the hand of the One who holds it.

verses.text: For I know the thoughts that I think toward you,” says Yahweh, “thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope and a future.
Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.
Therefore don’t be anxious for tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Each day’s own evil is sufficient.
Yahweh himself is who goes before you. He will be with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be discouraged.”
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me. Your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, to those who are called according to his purpose.

name: Calming a Child's Fears
note: Children's fears are real to them, and God takes them seriously — His "do not be afraid" is spoken tenderly, not impatiently. These verses are short enough for little ones to memorize and hold onto in the dark. Pray them together at bedtime.

verses.text: When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
In peace I will both lay myself down and sleep, for you, Yahweh alone, make me live in safety.
Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.”
He will not allow your foot to be moved. He who keeps you will not slumber. Behold, he who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.

name: Feeling Overwhelmed
note: When the waters rise, God does not hand you a lecture — He hands you Himself. He is a refuge, a rock higher than the flood, and a shepherd who restores. Come to Him with the whole pile; He sorts what you cannot.

verses.text: From the end of the earth, I will call to you, when my heart is overwhelmed. Lead me to the rock that is higher than I.

“Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.
When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they will not overflow you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned, and flame will not scorch you.

Yahweh is my shepherd: I shall lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul. He guides me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
He has said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.

name: Losing Someone You Love
note: Grief is love with nowhere to go, and God does not rush it — He stays close to the brokenhearted and keeps count of every tear. For those who hope in Christ, death is a comma, not a period. Sorrow is real; so is the reunion ahead.

verses.text: Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
Blessed are those who mourn, for they shall be comforted.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain, any more. The first things have passed away.”

But we don’t want you to be ignorant, brothers, concerning those who have fallen asleep, so that you don’t grieve like the rest, who have no hope. For if we believe that Jesus died and rose again, even so God will bring with him those who have fallen asleep in Jesus.
My flesh and my heart fails, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

“Don’t let your heart be troubled. Believe in God. Believe also in me. In my Father’s house are many homes. If it weren’t so, I would have told you. I am going to prepare a place for you. If I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and will receive you to myself; that where I am, you may be there also.

name: Grief After Losing a Grandparent
note: A grandparent's love leaves a deep, warm imprint — and a deep ache when they go. Let the family grieve together, tell their stories, and lean on the God who numbers our days and keeps His people forever. Their faithfulness can keep bearing fruit for generations.

verses.text: Precious in Yahweh’s sight is the death of his saints.
So teach us to count our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
having been reminded of the sincere faith that is in you; which lived first in your grandmother Lois, and your mother Eunice, and, I am persuaded, in you also.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain, any more. The first things have passed away.”
Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
A good man leaves an inheritance to his children’s children, but the wealth of the sinner is stored for the righteous.

name: Helping Kids Understand Death
note: Children need simple, honest words and a safe lap for their questions. Scripture gives both: Jesus wept at a grave, so tears are allowed — and He rose, so hope is honest too. Answer what they ask, comfort what they feel, and point gently to heaven.

verses.text: Jesus wept.
Jesus said to her, “I am the resurrection and the life. He who believes in me will still live, even if he dies.
Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
He will wipe away every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; neither will there be mourning, nor crying, nor pain, any more. The first things have passed away.”
He heals the broken in heart, and binds up their wounds.
But Jesus said, “Allow the little children, and don’t forbid them to come to me; for the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to ones like these.”

name: Losing a Family Pet
note: For a child, losing a pet is often their first real grief — treat it gently, not lightly. The God who made every creature and feeds every sparrow shares your family's tenderness toward what He made. It is good to thank Him together for the joy that pet brought.

verses.text: Yahweh is good to all. His tender mercies are over all his works.
“Aren’t two sparrows sold for an assarion coin? Not one of them falls on the ground apart from your Father’s will,
God made the animals of the earth after their kind, and the livestock after their kind, and everything that creeps on the ground after its kind. God saw that it was good.
Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
For every animal of the forest is mine, and the livestock on a thousand hills. I know all the birds of the mountains. The wild animals of the field are mine.
In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.

name: Controlling Your Anger
note: Anger itself is a signal, not always a sin — but unguided, it burns what we love. God offers a better way: slow it down, pray it out, and let His Spirit grow self-control where the fuse used to be. A gentle answer really does turn away wrath.

verses.text: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger; for the anger of man doesn’t produce the righteousness of God.
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
“Be angry, and don’t sin.” Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath,
One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.
Cease from anger, and forsake wrath. Don’t fret, it leads only to evildoing.
But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.

name: Patience with Your Family
note: The people we love most see our patience at its thinnest — and home is exactly where God wants to grow it thickest. His patience with you is the deep well to draw from when yours runs dry. Grace received becomes grace passed on.

verses.text: Put on therefore, as God’s chosen ones, holy and beloved, a heart of compassion, kindness, lowliness, humility, and perseverance; bearing with one another, and forgiving each other, if any man has a complaint against any; even as Christ forgave you, so you also do.
with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love;
Love is patient and is kind; love doesn’t envy. Love doesn’t brag, is not proud,
He who is slow to anger has great understanding, but he who has a quick temper displays folly.
Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.
Yahweh is merciful and gracious, slow to anger, and abundant in loving kindness.

name: Helping an Angry Child
note: Big feelings in little bodies need coaching, not just correction. These verses give children simple handles — be slow to get angry, use soft words, tell God about it — and give parents the patience to teach them a hundred times. God is gentle with us while we learn; we can be gentle while they do.

verses.text: So, then, my beloved brothers, let every man be swift to hear, slow to speak, and slow to anger;
A gentle answer turns away wrath, but a harsh word stirs up anger.
“Be angry, and don’t sin.” Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath,
Stand in awe, and don’t sin. Search your own heart on your bed, and be still. Selah.
Like a city that is broken down and without walls is a man whose spirit is without restraint.

In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.

name: Taming Your Tongue
note: Words can wound deeper than wounds and heal deeper than medicine — Scripture takes them that seriously. Ask God to set a guard over your mouth and to fill your home with words that give grace. The tongue is tamed one sentence at a time.

verses.text: Set a watch, Yahweh, before my mouth. Keep the door of my lips.
Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.
Pleasant words are a honeycomb, sweet to the soul, and health to the bones.
Death and life are in the power of the tongue; those who love it will eat its fruit.
Let your speech always be with grace, seasoned with salt, that you may know how you ought to answer each one.
With it we bless our God and Father, and with it we curse men, who are made in the image of God. Out of the same mouth comes blessing and cursing. My brothers, these things ought not to be so.

name: Anxiety About Finances
note: Jesus spoke directly to money worry: your Father knows what you need, feeds the birds, and clothes the flowers — and you matter far more. Bring the numbers to Him honestly, take wise next steps, and watch for daily bread. He has a long record of providing.

verses.text: “Therefore don’t be anxious, saying, ‘What will we eat?’, ‘What will we drink?’ or, ‘With what will we be clothed?’ For the Gentiles seek after all these things; for your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things. But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
See the birds of the sky, that they don’t sow, neither do they reap, nor gather into barns. Your heavenly Father feeds them. Aren’t you of much more value than they?
I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his children begging for bread.
Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.”
Honor Yahweh with your substance, with the first fruits of all your increase: so your barns will be filled with plenty, and your vats will overflow with new wine.

name: Contentment with What You Have
note: Contentment is not having everything you want — it is wanting what you already have, because God Himself is in it. Paul called it a secret he learned, which means it can be learned at your house too. Gratitude is the daily practice that gets you there.

verses.text: Not that I speak because of lack, for I have learned in whatever state I am, to be content in it. I know how to be humbled, and I know also how to abound. In everything and in all things I have learned the secret both to be filled and to be hungry, both to abound and to be in need. I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.
But godliness with contentment is great gain. For we brought nothing into the world, and we certainly can’t carry anything out. But having food and clothing, we will be content with that.
Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, “I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.”
Yahweh is my shepherd: I shall lack nothing.
He who loves silver shall not be satisfied with silver; nor he who loves abundance, with increase: this also is vanity.

“Don’t lay up treasures for yourselves on the earth, where moth and rust consume, and where thieves break through and steal; but lay up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where neither moth nor rust consume, and where thieves don’t break through and steal; for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.

name: Generosity & Giving
note: Giving is the family habit that most resembles God, who gave first and gave best. Scripture promises that generous people are enriched in every way that matters. Let your children watch you give gladly — it is a sermon they will never forget.

verses.text: Let each man give according as he has determined in his heart; not grudgingly, or under compulsion; for God loves a cheerful giver.
In all things I gave you an example, that so laboring you ought to help the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, that he himself said, ‘It is more blessed to give than to receive.’”
The liberal soul shall be made fat. He who waters shall be watered also himself.
“Give, and it will be given to you: good measure, pressed down, shaken together, and running over, will be given to you. For with the same measure you measure it will be measured back to you.”
He who has pity on the poor lends to Yahweh; he will reward him.

Bring the whole tithe into the storehouse, that there may be food in my house, and test me now in this,” says Yahweh of Armies, “if I will not open you the windows of heaven, and pour you out a blessing, that there will not be room enough for.

name: Job Loss & Providing
note: A lost job shakes more than a budget — it shakes identity and security. But your worth was never your paycheck, and your provider was never really the company. God who fed Israel in a wilderness can sustain your family through this in-between.

verses.text: My God will supply every need of yours according to his riches in glory in Christ Jesus.
I have been young, and now am old, yet I have not seen the righteous forsaken, nor his children begging for bread.

“Therefore don’t be anxious, saying, ‘What will we eat?’, ‘What will we drink?’ or, ‘With what will we be clothed?’ For the Gentiles seek after all these things; for your heavenly Father knows that you need all these things. But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
For I know the thoughts that I think toward you,” says Yahweh, “thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope and a future.
I will lift up my eyes to the hills. Where does my help come from? My help comes from Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.

name: When a Loved One Is Sick
note: Few things feel as helpless as watching someone you love suffer. But prayer is not nothing — it is the most powerful thing, reaching the Healer Himself. Bring your fear, keep showing up with love, and trust Him with what medicine cannot reach.

verses.text: Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord, and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
Praise Yahweh, my soul, and don’t forget all his benefits; who forgives all your sins; who heals all your diseases;
Heal me, O Yahweh, and I will be healed. Save me, and I will be saved; for you are my praise.
Yahweh will sustain him on his sickbed, and restore him from his bed of illness.

When evening came, they brought to him many possessed with demons. He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were sick; that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying, “He took our infirmities, and bore our diseases.”
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble.

name: Living with Ongoing Illness
note: When healing is slow or does not come, God has not looked away. His grace is sufficient for each day, His strength shows up best in weakness, and these light-and-momentary years are not the whole story. You are seen, sustained, and never alone in this.

verses.text: He has said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.
My flesh and my heart fails, but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day. For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory;

He gives power to the weak. He increases the strength of him who has no might. Even the youths faint and get weary, and the young men utterly fall; But those who wait for Yahweh will renew their strength. They will mount up with wings like eagles. They will run, and not be weary. They will walk, and not faint.
Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared with the glory which will be revealed toward us.

name: Praying for Healing
note: God invites bold, specific prayer for healing — Jesus never turned away a single person who asked. Pray with faith, welcome doctors and medicine as His common grace, and rest in His goodness whatever the timeline. The Lord who heals is also the Lord who holds.

verses.text: Heal me, O Yahweh, and I will be healed. Save me, and I will be saved; for you are my praise.

Is any among you sick? Let him call for the elders of the assembly, and let them pray over him, anointing him with oil in the name of the Lord, and the prayer of faith will heal him who is sick, and the Lord will raise him up. If he has committed sins, he will be forgiven.
Yahweh my God, I cried to you, and you have healed me.
Praise Yahweh, my soul, and don’t forget all his benefits; who forgives all your sins; who heals all your diseases;

and he said, “If you will diligently listen to Yahweh your God’s voice, and will do that which is right in his eyes, and will pay attention to his commandments, and keep all his statutes, I will put none of the diseases on you, which I have put on the Egyptians; for I am Yahweh who heals you.”
Jesus went about all the cities and the villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the Good News of the Kingdom, and healing every disease and every sickness among the people.

name: Strength for Caregivers
note: Caregiving is love poured out daily, often unseen — but God sees every act of it, and He counts it as service to Himself. He carries the carrier: come to Him for rest, ask for help without shame, and let others share the load.

verses.text: “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”
Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.

He gives power to the weak. He increases the strength of him who has no might. Even the youths faint and get weary, and the young men utterly fall; But those who wait for Yahweh will renew their strength. They will mount up with wings like eagles. They will run, and not be weary. They will walk, and not faint.
“The King will answer them, ‘Most certainly I tell you, because you did it to one of the least of these my brothers, you did it to me.’
Cast your burden on Yahweh, and he will sustain you. He will never allow the righteous to be moved.
And God is able to make all grace abound to you, that you, always having all sufficiency in everything, may abound to every good work.

name: Growing in Gratitude
note: Gratitude is the family habit that changes how everything else looks. Scripture ties thankfulness to peace, joy, and nearness to God — and it can be practiced at any dinner table, starting tonight.

verses.text: In everything give thanks, for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus toward you.
Give thanks to Yahweh, for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.
Enter into his gates with thanksgiving, into his courts with praise. Give thanks to him, and bless his name.
Whatever you do, in word or in deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father, through him.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow.
In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God.

name: Finding Joy Again
note: Joy in Scripture is not manufactured cheerfulness — it is the deep gladness of being near to God, and it can return even after long gray seasons. Ask Him to restore it; that is a prayer straight out of the Psalms, and one He loves to answer.

verses.text: Restore to me the joy of your salvation. Uphold me with a willing spirit.
You will show me the path of life. In your presence is fullness of joy. In your right hand there are pleasures forever more.

Then he said to them, “Go your way. Eat the fat, drink the sweet, and send portions to him for whom nothing is prepared, for today is holy to our Lord. Don’t be grieved, for the joy of Yahweh is your strength.”
I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full.
For his anger is but for a moment. His favor is for a lifetime. Weeping may stay for the night, but joy comes in the morning.
Now may the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, that you may abound in hope, in the power of the Holy Spirit.

name: Joy in Hard Seasons
note: Paul wrote "rejoice always" from a prison cell, so this is not advice from easy street. Joy that survives hard seasons is anchored in who God is, not how things are going. It often grows strongest right beside honest tears.

verses.text: Rejoice in the Lord always! Again I will say, “Rejoice!”

Count it all joy, my brothers, when you fall into various temptations, knowing that the testing of your faith produces endurance. Let endurance have its perfect work, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.

For though the fig tree doesn’t flourish, nor fruit be in the vines; the labor of the olive fails, the fields yield no food; the flocks are cut off from the fold, and there is no herd in the stalls: yet I will rejoice in Yahweh. I will be joyful in the God of my salvation!
Why are you in despair, my soul? Why are you disturbed within me? Hope in God! For I shall still praise him, the saving help of my countenance, and my God.

Not only this, but we also rejoice in our sufferings, knowing that suffering produces perseverance; and perseverance, proven character; and proven character, hope: and hope doesn’t disappoint us, because God’s love has been poured out into our hearts through the Holy Spirit who was given to us.

Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day. For our light affliction, which is for the moment, works for us more and more exceedingly an eternal weight of glory;

name: Celebrating as a Family
note: God built celebration into His people's calendar — feasts, songs, and stones of remembrance — because joy shared is joy multiplied and remembered. Mark your family's good days out loud and give the credit upward.

verses.text: This is the day that Yahweh has made. We will rejoice and be glad in it!
Yahweh has done great things for us, and we are glad.
Rejoice with those who rejoice. Weep with those who weep.
One generation will commend your works to another, and will declare your mighty acts.
Yahweh, your God, is among you, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with joy. He will calm you in his love. He will rejoice over you with singing.
Praise Yahweh, my soul, and don’t forget all his benefits;

name: My Child Is Being Bullied
keywords: kids are mean to my child
note: When your child is mistreated, God's heart moves first — He is a defender of the vulnerable and close to the crushed. Take real-world action, and anchor your child in this: their worth was settled by their Maker, not by the meanest voice at school.

verses.text: Yahweh is near to those who have a broken heart, and saves those who have a crushed spirit.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
I will give thanks to you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful. My soul knows that very well.
Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid or scared of them; for Yahweh your God himself is who goes with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you.”
Yahweh will also be a high tower for the oppressed; a high tower in times of trouble.
Don’t be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

name: Making New Friends
note: God designed people for friendship, and He cares about the lonely lunch table. These verses encourage the first brave hello — and remind us that to find a good friend, you start by being one. Pray together, by name, for the friends God is preparing.

verses.text: A man of many companions may be ruined, but there is a friend who sticks closer than a brother.

Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labor. For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow; but woe to him who is alone when he falls, and doesn’t have another to lift him up.
A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.
In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor preferring one another;
each of you not just looking to his own things, but each of you also to the things of others.
Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.”

name: Being a Good Friend
keywords: how to be a good friend
note: Scripture paints friendship as loyalty in all seasons, honesty that sharpens, and love that shows up. Jesus set the bar — He laid down His life for His friends — and He helps us love ours well.

verses.text: A friend loves at all times; and a brother is born for adversity.
Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
Iron sharpens iron; so a man sharpens his friend’s countenance.

Two are better than one, because they have a good reward for their labor. For if they fall, the one will lift up his fellow; but woe to him who is alone when he falls, and doesn’t have another to lift him up.
Rejoice with those who rejoice. Weep with those who weep.
Therefore exhort one another, and build each other up, even as you also do.

name: Friends Who Pull You Down
note: The Bible is honest: companions shape character, for better or worse. Choosing better friends (or kindly loosening unhealthy ones) is not unkind — it is wisdom. Pray for courage to walk in the right direction even when it means walking alone for a while.

verses.text: Don’t be deceived! “Evil companionships corrupt good morals.”
One who walks with wise men grows wise, but a companion of fools suffers harm.

Blessed is the man who doesn’t walk in the counsel of the wicked, nor stand on the path of sinners, nor sit in the seat of scoffers; but his delight is in Yahweh’s law. On his law he meditates day and night.
Don’t enter into the path of the wicked. Don’t walk in the way of evil men. Avoid it, and don’t pass by it. Turn from it, and pass on.
Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.
Flee from youthful lusts; but pursue righteousness, faith, love, and peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart.

name: Teaching Kids to Obey
note: Obedience is taught best as trust, not just compliance — kids obey parents they know are for them, the same way we obey a God we know loves us. Be consistent, be calm, and let your own glad obedience to God be the loudest lesson.

verses.text: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right. “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with a promise: “that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.”
Train up a child in the way he should go, and when he is old he will not depart from it.
Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord.

These words, which I command you today, shall be on your heart; and you shall teach them diligently to your children, and shall talk of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk by the way, and when you lie down, and when you rise up.
Correct your son, and he will give you peace; yes, he will bring delight to your soul.
But he said, “On the contrary, blessed are those who hear the word of God, and keep it.”

name: Growing Self-Control
note: Self-control is listed as fruit of the Spirit — which means it grows from God's help, not white-knuckled effort alone. A person who rules their own spirit, Scripture says, is mightier than a conqueror of cities. Start small, pray often, and celebrate progress.

verses.text: But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law.
Like a city that is broken down and without walls is a man whose spirit is without restraint.
For God didn’t give us a spirit of fear, but of power, love, and self-control.
One who is slow to anger is better than the mighty; one who rules his spirit, than he who takes a city.
Every man who strives in the games exercises self-control in all things. Now they do it to receive a corruptible crown, but we an incorruptible.

For the grace of God has appeared, bringing salvation to all men, instructing us to the intent that, denying ungodliness and worldly lusts, we would live soberly, righteously, and godly in this present age;

name: Respect Between Parents & Kids
note: Honor flows both ways in God's design: children honor parents, and parents treat children with dignity that makes honor easy to give. These verses set the tone for a home where respect is the house language.

verses.text: Children, obey your parents in the Lord, for this is right. “Honor your father and mother,” which is the first commandment with a promise: “that it may be well with you, and you may live long on the earth.”
“Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land which Yahweh your God gives you.
Children, obey your parents in all things, for this pleases the Lord. Fathers, don’t provoke your children, so that they won’t be discouraged.
My son, listen to your father’s instruction, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching: for they will be a garland to grace your head, and chains around your neck.
Honor all men. Love the brotherhood. Fear God. Honor the king.
doing nothing through rivalry or through conceit, but in humility, each counting others better than himself;

name: Building Good Habits
note: Faithfulness in small daily things is the Bible's growth strategy — little by little, line upon line, day after day. A family habit of prayer and Scripture, even five minutes, compounds into something mighty over a year. Just do not give up on day four.

verses.text: Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up.

This book of the law shall not depart from your mouth, but you shall meditate on it day and night, that you may observe to do according to all that is written in it; for then you shall make your way prosperous, and then you shall have good success.

but his delight is in Yahweh’s law. On his law he meditates day and night. He will be like a tree planted by the streams of water, that produces its fruit in its season, whose leaf also does not wither. Whatever he does shall prosper.
He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit; and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.

Brothers, I don’t regard myself as yet having taken hold, but one thing I do. Forgetting the things which are behind, and stretching forward to the things which are before, I press on toward the goal for the prize of the high calling of God in Christ Jesus.

name: Wrestling with Doubt
note: Doubt is not the opposite of faith — it is often faith asking honest questions. Jesus met Thomas's doubts with open hands, not a lecture, and He welcomes yours the same way. Bring the questions to Him rather than away from Him.

verses.text: Immediately the father of the child cried out with tears, “I believe. Help my unbelief!”

Then he said to Thomas, “Reach here your finger, and see my hands. Reach here your hand, and put it into my side. Don’t be unbelieving, but believing.” Thomas answered him, “My Lord and my God!” Jesus said to him, “Because you have seen me, you have believed. Blessed are those who have not seen, and have believed.”

How long, Yahweh? Will you forget me forever? How long will you hide your face from me? How long shall I take counsel in my soul, having sorrow in my heart every day? How long shall my enemy triumph over me?
Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.
Oh taste and see that Yahweh is good. Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him.
On some have compassion, making a distinction,

name: Growing Your Faith
note: Faith grows like a tree by a river — slowly, steadily, fed by Word, prayer, and practice. God promises that those who seek Him find Him, and that He finishes what He starts in every believer, kids included.

verses.text: So faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.

but his delight is in Yahweh’s law. On his law he meditates day and night. He will be like a tree planted by the streams of water, that produces its fruit in its season, whose leaf also does not wither. Whatever he does shall prosper.
You shall seek me, and find me, when you search for me with all your heart.
being confident of this very thing, that he who began a good work in you will complete it until the day of Jesus Christ.
But grow in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. To him be the glory both now and forever. Amen.
As therefore you received Christ Jesus, the Lord, walk in him, rooted and built up in him, and established in the faith, even as you were taught, abounding in it in thanksgiving.

name: Answering Kids' Questions About God
keywords: why does God allow bad things
note: A child's hard question is a doorway, not a danger — Jesus welcomed children and their wonderings. You do not need perfect answers; "what a great question, let's explore it together" teaches them that faith and curiosity are friends.

verses.text: But Jesus said, “Allow the little children, and don’t forbid them to come to me; for the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to ones like these.”

These words, which I command you today, shall be on your heart; and you shall teach them diligently to your children, and shall talk of them when you sit in your house, and when you walk by the way, and when you lie down, and when you rise up.
One generation will commend your works to another, and will declare your mighty acts.

Yes, if you call out for discernment, and lift up your voice for understanding; If you seek her as silver, and search for her as for hidden treasures: then you will understand the fear of Yahweh, and find the knowledge of God.

“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, and your ways are not my ways,” says Yahweh. “For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways, and my thoughts than your thoughts.
From infancy, you have known the holy Scriptures which are able to make you wise for salvation through faith, which is in Christ Jesus.

name: Trusting God's Plan
note: God's plans unfold on His clock, often through detours we would never choose — Joseph's prison preceded his palace. Trust does not require understanding the route; it requires knowing the Driver. He is working all of it for good.

verses.text: Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.
For I know the thoughts that I think toward you,” says Yahweh, “thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope and a future.
We know that all things work together for good for those who love God, to those who are called according to his purpose.

“For my thoughts are not your thoughts, and your ways are not my ways,” says Yahweh. “For as the heavens are higher than the earth, so are my ways higher than your ways, and my thoughts than your thoughts.
I will instruct you and teach you in the way which you shall go. I will counsel you with my eye on you.
He has made everything beautiful in its time. He has also set eternity in their hearts, yet so that man can’t find out the work that God has done from the beginning even to the end.

name: Moving to a New Home
note: Moving uproots more than furniture — it uproots hearts, especially small ones. But God goes ahead of every moving truck: He is already in the new neighborhood, preparing friends and places you cannot see yet. Home, ultimately, is wherever He is with you.

verses.text: Yahweh himself is who goes before you. He will be with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be discouraged.”
Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.”
Yahweh will keep your going out and your coming in, from this time forward, and forever more.
Behold, I am with you, and will keep you, wherever you go, and will bring you again into this land. For I will not leave you, until I have done that which I have spoken of to you.”
For I know the thoughts that I think toward you,” says Yahweh, “thoughts of peace, and not of evil, to give you hope and a future.
Lord, you have been our dwelling place for all generations.

name: Starting a New School
note: First days are big days for small hearts. These verses fit in a backpack — short, sturdy promises that God walks every hallway with them. Pray them at the breakfast table, and send your child in known and loved.

verses.text: Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.”
When I am afraid, I will put my trust in you.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.
Yahweh will keep your going out and your coming in, from this time forward, and forever more.
Trust in Yahweh with all your heart, and don’t lean on your own understanding. In all your ways acknowledge him, and he will make your paths straight.

name: Welcoming a New Baby
note: Every child is knit together by God and arrives as a gift, not an interruption. Amid the joy and the exhaustion, these verses help the whole family — including brand-new big siblings — welcome this little one as God's good idea.

verses.text: For you formed my inmost being. You knit me together in my mother’s womb. I will give thanks to you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful. My soul knows that very well.
Behold, children are a heritage of Yahweh. The fruit of the womb is his reward.
Every good gift and every perfect gift is from above, coming down from the Father of lights, with whom can be no variation, nor turning shadow.
I prayed for this child; and Yahweh has given me my petition which I asked of him.
He will feed his flock like a shepherd. He will gather the lambs in his arm, and carry them in his bosom. He will gently lead those who have their young.
Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me,

name: Navigating Big Changes
note: Seasons change; God does not. When life rearranges itself around you, His character is the furniture that stays bolted down — same mercy, same faithfulness, same love. He makes new paths through every wilderness.

verses.text: Behold, I will do a new thing. It springs out now. Don’t you know it? I will even make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.
Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever.
For everything there is a season, and a time for every purpose under heaven:
It is because of Yahweh’s loving kindnesses that we are not consumed, because his compassion doesn’t fail. They are new every morning. Great is your faithfulness.
God is our refuge and strength, a very present help in trouble. Therefore we won’t be afraid, though the earth changes, though the mountains are shaken into the heart of the seas;
“For I, Yahweh, don’t change; therefore you, sons of Jacob, are not consumed.

name: Feeling Lonely
note: Loneliness tells you that you are unseen — and it is lying. The God who knows your name, counts your hairs, and never sleeps is nearer than your own breath. From that secure company, He often leads us gently toward new connection.

verses.text: Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid or scared of them; for Yahweh your God himself is who goes with you. He will not fail you nor forsake you.”

Where could I go from your Spirit? Or where could I flee from your presence? If I ascend up into heaven, you are there. If I make my bed in Sheol, behold, you are there! If I take the wings of the dawn, and settle in the uttermost parts of the sea; Even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me.
Don’t you be afraid, for I am with you. Don’t be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you. Yes, I will help you. Yes, I will uphold you with the right hand of my righteousness.
teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.
Turn to me, and have mercy on me, for I am desolate and afflicted.
God sets the lonely in families. He brings out the prisoners with singing, but the rebellious dwell in a sun-scorched land.

name: Feeling Left Out
note: Being left out stings at any age — and Jesus knows the feeling personally; He was rejected by His own town. In God's family there is no outside circle: you are chosen, wanted, and welcomed at the best table there is.

verses.text: But you are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, a people for God’s own possession, that you may proclaim the excellence of him who called you out of darkness into his marvelous light:
When my father and my mother forsake me, then Yahweh will take me up.
All those whom the Father gives me will come to me. He who comes to me I will in no way throw out.

even as he chose us in him before the foundation of the world, that we would be holy and without defect before him in love; having predestined us for adoption as children through Jesus Christ to himself, according to the good pleasure of his desire,
I will give thanks to you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made. Your works are wonderful. My soul knows that very well.

For I am persuaded that neither death, nor life, nor angels, nor principalities, nor things present, nor things to come, nor powers, nor height, nor depth, nor any other created thing, will be able to separate us from God’s love, which is in Christ Jesus our Lord.

name: Parenting on Your Own
note: Parenting alone is heavy, and God sees every unseen sacrifice. He promises to be father to the fatherless, defender and helper of the one carrying the load — your kids have a heavenly Parent watching over them with you. Ask for help; receiving it is grace, not failure.

verses.text: A father of the fatherless, and a defender of the widows, is God in his holy habitation.
He will feed his flock like a shepherd. He will gather the lambs in his arm, and carry them in his bosom. He will gently lead those who have their young.
I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.
I will lift up my eyes to the hills. Where does my help come from? My help comes from Yahweh, who made heaven and earth.
He has said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Most gladly therefore I will rather glory in my weaknesses, that the power of Christ may rest on me.
“Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.

name: God Is With You
note: The Bible's most repeated promise is also its sweetest: I am with you. Not "I am watching from a distance" — with you, in the kitchen, the hospital room, the school bus. Whatever today holds, you do not walk through it alone.

verses.text: teaching them to observe all things that I commanded you. Behold, I am with you always, even to the end of the age.” Amen.
Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death, I will fear no evil, for you are with me. Your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they will not overflow you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be burned, and flame will not scorch you.
Yahweh, your God, is among you, a mighty one who will save. He will rejoice over you with joy. He will calm you in his love. He will rejoice over you with singing.

Where could I go from your Spirit? Or where could I flee from your presence? If I ascend up into heaven, you are there. If I make my bed in Sheol, behold, you are there! If I take the wings of the dawn, and settle in the uttermost parts of the sea; Even there your hand will lead me, and your right hand will hold me.
Haven’t I commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Don’t be afraid. Don’t be dismayed, for Yahweh your God is with you wherever you go.”

name: Resisting Temptation
note: No temptation is unique to you, and none is unbeatable — God always builds an exit door, and Jesus, who was tempted Himself, helps those being tempted. Memorizing Scripture is the family sword for these battles; it is the one Jesus used.

verses.text: No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

For we don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities, but one who has been in all points tempted like we are, yet without sin. Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and may find grace for help in time of need.
But he answered, “It is written, ‘Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that proceeds out of the mouth of God.’”
Be subject therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.
I have hidden your word in my heart, that I might not sin against you.
But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.

name: Breaking a Bad Habit
note: Old patterns break the way new ones form: daily, with God's power and honest companions. You are not your habit — in Christ you are a new creation, and His grace meets every relapse with mercy that gets you up again. Freedom is usually a road, not a door.

verses.text: Therefore if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old things have passed away. Behold, all things have become new.
Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.
I can do all things through Christ, who strengthens me.

No temptation has taken you except what is common to man. God is faithful, who will not allow you to be tempted above what you are able, but will with the temptation also make the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.
Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage.
for a righteous man falls seven times, and rises up again; but the wicked are overthrown by calamity.

name: Guarding Your Heart & Mind
note: What flows into a heart eventually flows out of a life — which is why Scripture posts a guard at the gate. As a family, choose what you watch, scroll, and dwell on the way you choose food: is it true, lovely, and worth growing on?

verses.text: Keep your heart with all diligence, for out of it is the wellspring of life.

Finally, brothers, whatever things are true, whatever things are honorable, whatever things are just, whatever things are pure, whatever things are lovely, whatever things are of good report; if there is any virtue, and if there is any praise, think about these things.
I will set no vile thing before my eyes. I hate the deeds of faithless men. They will not cling to me.
Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.
Set your mind on the things that are above, not on the things that are on the earth.
Turn my eyes away from looking at worthless things. Revive me in your ways.

name: Integrity When No One Is Watching
keywords: doing right when no one sees
note: Integrity is who you are in the dark — and it is the pillow Scripture says lets you sleep secure. God sees what no one else sees, and He honors the quiet, unrewarded right choice. Walk straight; the path holds.

verses.text: He who walks blamelessly walks surely, but he who perverts his ways will be found out.
Yahweh, who shall dwell in your sanctuary? Who shall live on your holy hill? He who walks blamelessly does what is right, and speaks truth in his heart;
Lying lips are an abomination to Yahweh, but those who do the truth are his delight.
He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much. He who is dishonest in a very little is also dishonest in much.
And whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord, and not for men,
Search me, God, and know my heart. Try me, and know my thoughts. See if there is any wicked way in me, and lead me in the everlasting way.

name: Burnout & Exhaustion
note: Even prophets burned out — Elijah collapsed under a tree, and God's response was sleep, food, and gentle care before any sermon. Your weariness is not weakness or faithlessness. Come to Jesus for rest first; direction can come after the nap.

verses.text: “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest. Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls. For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

He lay down and slept under a juniper tree; and behold, an angel touched him, and said to him, “Arise and eat!” He looked, and behold, there was at his head a cake baked on the coals, and a jar of water. He ate and drank, and lay down again. Yahweh’s angel came again the second time, and touched him, and said, “Arise and eat, because the journey is too great for you.”

He gives power to the weak. He increases the strength of him who has no might. Even the youths faint and get weary, and the young men utterly fall; But those who wait for Yahweh will renew their strength. They will mount up with wings like eagles. They will run, and not be weary. They will walk, and not faint.

Yahweh is my shepherd: I shall lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul. He guides me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
My soul rests in God alone. My salvation is from him.
Therefore we don’t faint, but though our outward man is decaying, yet our inward man is renewed day by day.

name: Learning to Rest
note: Rest is not a reward for finishing everything — it is a rhythm God wove into creation and modeled Himself. A family that practices stopping (even imperfectly) teaches its children that they are loved for who they are, not what they produce.

verses.text: On the seventh day God finished his work which he had done; and he rested on the seventh day from all his work which he had done. God blessed the seventh day, and made it holy, because he rested in it from all his work of creation which he had done.
“Remember the Sabbath day, to keep it holy.
He said to them, “You come apart into a deserted place, and rest awhile.” For there were many coming and going, and they had no leisure so much as to eat.
He makes me lie down in green pastures. He leads me beside still waters. He restores my soul. He guides me in the paths of righteousness for his name’s sake.
“Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.
It is vain for you to rise up early, to stay up late, eating the bread of toil; for he gives sleep to his loved ones.

name: An Overloaded Family Schedule
note: Busyness can crowd out the very things the schedule was supposed to serve. Jesus praised Mary for choosing the one needed thing over many hurried ones. It may be time to ask together: what is God actually asking of our family this season — and what can go?

verses.text: Jesus answered her, “Martha, Martha, you are anxious and troubled about many things, but one thing is needed. Mary has chosen the good part, which will not be taken away from her.”
“Be still, and know that I am God. I will be exalted among the nations. I will be exalted in the earth.”
Better is a handful, with quietness, than two handfuls with labor and chasing after wind.
But seek first God’s Kingdom, and his righteousness; and all these things will be given to you as well.
So teach us to count our days, that we may gain a heart of wisdom.
The plans of the diligent surely lead to profit; and everyone who is hasty surely rushes to poverty.

name: Anxious Nights & Sleep
note: The middle of the night is where worries grow loudest — and where God's promises are made for. He gives sleep to those He loves and stays awake on watch so you do not have to. Hand Him the night shift.

verses.text: In peace I will both lay myself down and sleep, for you, Yahweh alone, make me live in safety.
It is vain for you to rise up early, to stay up late, eating the bread of toil; for he gives sleep to his loved ones.
He will not allow your foot to be moved. He who keeps you will not slumber. Behold, he who keeps Israel will neither slumber nor sleep.
When you lie down, you will not be afraid. Yes, you will lie down, and your sleep will be sweet.

In nothing be anxious, but in everything, by prayer and petition with thanksgiving, let your requests be made known to God. And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your thoughts in Christ Jesus.
Therefore don’t be anxious for tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Each day’s own evil is sufficient.
